mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals a sharp contraction in household income and consumer demand, forcing equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and real personal spending indicate a synchronized collapse in residential investment and consumer demand, which directly reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 55% in matched historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction and factory output weakness, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where industrial production contracts, invalidating the supply-contraction risk-off channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces markets to anticipate tighter monetary policy and higher real discount rates, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, indicating inflation is priced as nominal growth rather than policy risk.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly contractions in real GDP and nonfarm payrolls confirm a broad-based economic downturn, eliminating growth optionality for equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and payroll decline fails to predict negative returns, with positive 60-day returns occurring in more than 50% of overlapping quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in real personal spending directly boost corporate revenue forecasts and earnings growth expectations, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in fewer than 45% of months with rising real spending.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.5%, declining housing starts are offset by cheaper mortgage financing and liquidity injections, which stabilizes financial sector balance sheets and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Low rates combined with falling housing starts produce negative returns in more than 65% of cases, indicating liquidity fails to offset real estate sector distress.
